Chip Manufacturing.name: Chip Manufacturing
Chip Manufacturing.description: Chip Manufacturing offers research opportunities in the areas of emerging electronic devices and systems beyond Moore's law, logic and memory semiconductors for artificial intelligence, neuromorphic computing devices and systems, wide bandgap semiconductors for electric vehicles and smart grid, radiation-hard and cryogenic semiconductors for space applications, 3D heterogeneous integration for RF

Communications and Networks.name: Communications and Networks
Communications and Networks.description: The Communications and Networks research group focuses on advancing the theory, design, and implementation of modern communication systems and networks. Research areas include information and coding theory, Artificial Intelligence (AI)-native next-generation (NextG) communication networks and systems, wireless and optical networking, and edge intelligence for decision-making. The group explores al

Computer Engineering and Systems.name: Computer Engineering and Systems
Computer Engineering and Systems.description: Computer Engineering offers research opportunities in the areas of computer communications and networks, data science, multimedia, storage systems, parallel and distributed computing and architecture, fault-tolerant computing and design for testing,

Device Science and Nanotechnology.name: Device Science and Nanotechnology
Device Science and Nanotechnology.description: The Device Science and Nanotechnology program in the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering at Texas A&M University encompasses a wide range of research topics from electro-optics to quantum computing. The electro-optics program incorporates a range of technologies that use optical and electronic phenomena. Research areas of primary interest include fiber optics, integrated optics, and

Electromagnetics and Microwaves.name: Electromagnetics and Microwaves
Electromagnetics and Microwaves.description: Research activities in electromagnetics and microwaves span a broad spectrum of applications. This includes the theoretical and experimental aspects of antennas, electromagnetic theory, electromagnetic wave scattering, active and passive microwave and millimeter-wave circuits, linear and non-linear optical or microwave guiding systems, and microstrip antennas.

Energy and Power.name: Energy and Power
Energy and Power.description: Faculty expertise from both power systems and power electronics is frequently combined in research activities. Power systems research is performed in the areas of analysis, reliability, monitoring, control, and protection of power systems. Some faculty have a strong interest in control systems, digital signal processing, data communications, and intelligent system applications. Power electronics r

Information Science and Learning Systems.name: Information Science and Learning Systems
Information Science and Learning Systems.description: Research activities in the Information Science and Learning Systems program are focused on advancing the state of the art in areas including information theory, coding theory, data compression, detection and estimation, receiver signal processing, networking coding and network information theory, multimedia security, secrecy systems, optimization techniques, robust control, adaptive control and co

Security.name: Security
Security.description: Security research includes computer security, cyber-physical security, hardware security, cryptography, secure computing architectures, smart-grid security, privacy-enhancing technologies, risk assessment, and supply-chain security.
